text.title: Traveling Europe Alone With Absolutely No Plan
text.u: I will see you on the other side.
[sighs and gasps] All right.
[laughter] It sounds cliche cuz I'm sure
you've heard a million Tik Toks saying
the same dumb [ __ ] but please, like,
everyone says it cuz it's true. You are
so much more capable than you think you
are. Trust me. I told myself I would go
frolicking by myself at least once. I
guess a lesson that like people
sometimes don't really realize. It's
like there's no reason to freak out in
any case scenario.
You absolutely 100% underestimate your
ability to just figure it out.
I just I never I never thought I
couldn't travel to Europe by myself.
[music] It was just a matter of whether
I actually pursued it or not. [singing]
The plan was simple. to fly to
Switzerland on September 1st, take a
train to a town called Interlockin, and
from there wing it. The goal to explore
the continent of Europe for two months
completely alone with no plan.
This place is gorgeous and it's like
literally just Zurich. It's just a city.
It's not any It's I'm not even in the
Alps yet. This place is awesome. But
this is day one. This is hostel number
one. I'm leaving this hostel on Friday
to go to the Alps.
After two quick days in the city of
Zurich, I hopped on my first big train
headed for interlocking.
I got to my hostel in the afternoon,
like 3:00 p.m.ish.
My back hurts. I was happier than ever
to be in the mountains finally,
of course, until the very next morning
when I hit my first major mental
challenge.
made it to Louder Vernon. It is the 6th.
This place is It's cool. Uh I'm just I'm
struggling. I This is a little bit
harder than I thought it was going to
be, to be honest with you. The hostel
I'm staying in is cool. There's cool
people. It's a cool little hostel. But
the bunk room, like where I'm sleeping,
it is. There's a part of me that's like
screaming at me like, I want to go home.
I want to go home. I want to go home.
It's literally only been like 5 days,
but I I don't want to go home
empty-handed. That's ultimately what I'm
trying to say. Like I came here to
figure figure some [ __ ] out. And so when
I start feeling these things, it makes
me feel like I'm on the right path, but
it [ __ ] sucks. I don't know, man. I'm
going to just wrap this up cuz it's been
really fun. Yeah, I've thought about it
a lot since that day. I think the only
thing that was really bringing me any
sort of comfort in that moment in time
was that I knew that the sort of pain I
was feeling was my growth happening in
real time.
Are you kidding me? I decided to start
walking from Louderder Burnon to a town
called Merin. And this is turning out to
be like the best decision I've made on
the whole trip so far. I I I keep
stressing out about like planning out
decision, decision, like what's next?
What's next? What's next? Like, but then
I was like, you know what? [ __ ] it. I'm
going to just do it. I will figure out
how to get back later. I also just like
don't really care right now cuz I'm kind
of in heaven. [music]
>> [music]
>> Man, yeah, that hike was emotional.
[music]
>> I was really struggling that morning and
I didn't really know what to do. [music]
I was scared to make a move because I
didn't [music] know really how I would
get back.
>> And that's when I realized it would
probably be better if I just jumped and
I'll figure out how to get back later.
And that was the mindset that led me to
some of the most beautiful places I had
ever seen
to meet some of the coolest people I had
ever met.
>> A classic a classic football
>> throughout the rest of this two-month
adventure.
>> About to eat [ __ ]
>> One of the main destinations that I
really wanted to see along this trip was
the Doommites located in northern Italy.
uh they were going to be kind of
difficult to get to from where I was in
Switzerland. So I decided to take a
couple stops along the way. One of which
was a place called Chinqutere. I'm
currently in Laspzia, Italy um in
Chikatere. It's been such an interesting
experience so far. I I have Yeah. I
don't know. I It's like I'm starting to
realize how capable I actually am of
doing this stuff. [music]
>> [music]
[music]
[music]
>> Shutter was the first [music]
solo like completely solo stay of the
trip. I wasn't in a hostel. I was in a
private room [music] for the first time
in like two weeks, which is really nice.
But I mean, it definitely was a little
bit lonely at times, but I was still
kind of just [music] in dreamland
because I was just doing this by myself.
[music] So, I I felt pretty good. The
only thing with Chin with Terry was this
place was packed with people. I did a
couple hikes drenched in sweat. There
was a lot of people on the hikes and
ultimately it wasn't my favorite place
uh by any means for the whole trip. But
I'm glad I stopped there at the very
least because it broke up the trip
[music] to the next stop which was the
Doommites which was probably the best
stop of the entire trip. But I wouldn't
get there [music] before probably one of
the most stressful days of my entire
life.
>> First like kind of legitimate problem.
my literal first train of the [ __ ]
day got uh delayed. And so now I'm like
kind of screwed in a way. I was supposed
to get to the town that I'm going to in
the Doommites at like 300 p.m. And now
I'm not going to get there till like
7:30. Well, I'm not even I'm not going
to get to a town called Bzano until
7:30. And then it's an hour and a half
car ride from Bzano to the town I'm
trying to get to. And there are no
trains and no buses by the time I'm
going to get there. So, my only options
are to rent a car or get an extremely
expensive Uber or taxi. And I've been
sitting at this train station for like
an hour. This sucks, but uh it's going
to be a long day. I figured it was going
to be a long day, but this is even
longer than I was hoping. Since I had
five trainers, that means I would be
obviously switching trains five times,
which means if one of the trains was too
late, I would miss the rest of the
trains. And the very first train of the
of the day was like 40 minutes late,
screwing up my entire day. I have been
Oh my god. Yeah, we're doing this. I
have been on trains for the last 12
hours. We made it not to the hotel that
I'm supposed to stay in. Actually, I was
supposed to take a bus from Bzano to Val
Gardina, but the last bus was at 7:25
and my train got in at 7:30. The two
options were Ubers or um I spend
[snorts] the night at the train station.
So I decided to do neither of those
things and get an emergency hotel in
Bolzano. Cost me $170 for the night.
Today was a stressful day. I'm not going
to lie. It was stressful and I feel
actually like really amazing that I'm
here and alive and breathing. Tomorrow I
take the hour and a half bus to Val
Gardina. And I hope I don't [ __ ] that
up. But the good news is that if I do
[ __ ] it up, I have the entire day to
figure it out. So 10:00 a.m. the next
morning, I took the bus straight to the
town I was staying in called Val
Gardina, right in the middle of the
Doommites. And it quickly became
apparent to me that the suffering I had
endured the day prior was completely
worth it.
>> I'm just now seeing the room that I
booked here a few days ago in the
Doommites. Dude,
are you kidding me? What have I done?
I'm here for four nights. I think it's
safe to say that like the entire
shenanigans that I had to go through
yesterday and today was worth it cuz
holy [ __ ] I'm so glad to be back in the
mountains, too. [music]
[music]
>> [music]
>> What? What? I'm pretty sure I actually
just died in an [music] elevator. I was
still alone, but this time it was
different. This wasn't loneliness. It
was solitude. I didn't speak to very
many people over those 3 days, but I was
happier than I had ever been.
>> I told myself I would go frolicking by
myself at least once on this trip. I
logged over like 30,000 [music] steps on
all three days in the Doommites. I hiked
up thousands of feet. My body was
screaming at me, but I didn't hear it.
[music]
Every second I got to myself out in
these [music] mountains, I was convinced
I was the happiest person on earth. This
was the most beautiful place I had ever
seen.
>> Look at that.
>> [music]
>> I went through some serious challenges
and spent a lot more money than I wanted
to in order to get here. But I can
confidently say that every second of
stress, every penny spent was completely
worth it.
All right, today is day 22.
I don't know when the [ __ ] that
happened. I made it to Insrook, Austria
yesterday. There's really no like
objective today at all which um
definitely is [ __ ] with my head as it
usually does when I try to not do
anything for a day. I feel depressed.
Something I did realize in the doommites
actually kind of enjoy my own company a
lot more than I thought I did. Like I
don't know back at home I always felt
like it was definitely getting to a
point where I started to kind of like
hate myself for some reason. Whether it
was because I couldn't do things I
wanted to do or thought I wasn't good
enough or whatever it was. I kind of
just like didn't like myself and didn't
really like who I was, I guess. I don't
know. But here, I still feel like a lot
more comfortable with myself, if that
makes any sense. Which is just a weird
feeling cuz I guess I've never really
felt that way before. Like I feel more
like secure and like a little bit more
confident in my own skin.
[music]
I think Insbrook was the first time that
I actually started to miss home.
[music]
Up until that point, I had been pretty
decently distracted by everything and
still adjusting to the life.
>> Yeah. [music]
>> Yeah. I remember sitting down on that
bench and didn't have much of an
objective that day and I definitely was
starting to think about home a little
bit more than weeks past.
But on the second day, I was able to uh
spend the day up in the mountains again,
which made me feel a lot better. [music]
I went up to the top of Insbrook, which
is a mountain that's at about I think
It was nice to forget that feeling for a
day.
This is ridiculous.
>> Yeah. The the more you sit still, the
more everything catches up to you, you
know, start thinking about my dog. And
um you know, when you're in the
mountains, it doesn't really matter how
homesick you may or may not be. Like,
you don't really think about it. You
don't really care. But uh on the on the
in the quiet moments
in between,
it definitely starts to hit you.
The next stop after that was Salsburg,
but Salsburg was really just like a home
base in order for me to go visit
Hallstat.
[music]
[music]
Holstat is a crazy little town buried in
the mountains on a lake only accessible
by boat.
>> I was in Salsburg for
three full days. I think this [music]
was definitely the highlight of that
little excursion.
[music]
This was another one of those touristy
spots. When you look up places to see in
Austria, the first thing that's going to
come up is probably H Hallstat. So,
there was a fair amount of people there,
but I tried to make do with the
experience as much as as well as I
could.
>> Today is
day 28. I'm feeling something I never
thought I would feel. Um, I'm bored.
Like I'm not even I'm literally bored
for the past like five days. I've just
been like I don't know. I I I'm like I
don't like I'm going to Budapest
tomorrow, so Budapest will be cool. I'm
there for 2 days, but the cities to me
are just like and I know Budapest is
supposed to be really cool, I guess. But
I don't know. And little did I know I
could not have been more wrong. The next
few days would be some of the most
challenging of the entire trip.
Today is October
1st. I've been in Europe for 30 days.
Um, I'd love to tell you that I'm like
having such a great time and this is so
cool, but the past like
week has been kind of [ __ ] and the past
like two days in particular, today
included, definitely not um having a
great time. I don't know. I just have
not been enjoying myself here at all and
I haven't met anyone here either. I've
tried a a couple times to talk to people
and meet people in my own hostel to no
avail. So, I was going to go to Vienna
and then Prague. I'm still going to
Vienna cuz I already booked it. But
honestly, I just I don't think I'm going
to go to Prague. I dead ass think I'm
just going to go back to [ __ ]
Switzerland, dude. I genuinely think
where I belong is the mountains. I'm
just going to go back to the mountains.
I do not like cities.
Even if they're surrounded by mountains,
I don't really like them. Budapest was
the first time that I think I like
really really struggled. My hostel was
this weird like Squid Game looking
place. The it was hot at night, so I was
sweating in my sleep. The bathroom was
on the other side of the the building.
It it just the whole thing was kind of a
disaster. And I was really concerned
because I my plan was to go to Vienna
and then Prague and then Munich and
those are all cities. And it was very
clear to me at this point that I did not
like cities. But regardless of this, I
decided to just follow through on my
plan anyways. And that's when I ran into
an absolute disaster of a 4-hour train
ride.
We made it to Prague. It's October
6. I can't believe it's been over a
month at this point. Uh yesterday I took
the train from Vienna to Prague which
was 4 hours and I didn't have a reserved
seat cuz you didn't need one but it
recommended it but they were all sold
out so I wound up on the floor for 4
hours. Great stuff. Lost feeling in my
legs but I got here and it was raining.
That sucked. And um there's a just a
great nice old gentleman in my six bed
dorm here in Prague that snores like I
don't even like a sailor. It is so oof.
And on top of that, he loves to make
lots of funny noises in the middle of
the night. So regardless of my sleep-d
deprived and sick state, I actually feel
pretty good cuz I only have one day here
in Prague cuz I'm getting sick of cities
to be honest with you. Although I will
say Prague is pretty sick. Vienna was
also pretty cool. Vienna was actually
not that bad cuz I met some cool people
and spent the day with them.
[music]
>> [music]
[music]
[music]
>> I booked my flight home for October 31st
from London. So, I got to find my way
over there. I see the end of the tunnel.
Not that this is a [music] dark, scary
tunnel necessarily, but I I see the end
of the end of the road, which is
comforting for sure. Has definitely had
a bit of an impact [music] on my my
mental state knowing that there's an end
now. I just think that the last few last
couple [music] weeks have been like
Vienna was nice cuz I got to hang out
with some people. Prague is cool. I'm by
myself, but I'm only here for a day and
[music] it's actually kind of cool here.
And I'm going to Munich tomorrow for
only a day. And then after that, I'm I
said, "Fuck it. I'm going back into the
mountains. I'm going back to Austria and
then Switzerland and then uh Shamani."
>> Yeah. I don't know. I don't know how to
describe it. I'm I'm I'm definitely
looking forward to going home, but I
know damn well I'm going to miss every
sec. Even the even the moments in
Budapest when I was [ __ ] miserable.
Like I'm going to miss that when I get
there. I'm trying to remind myself that.
Like no matter how much my feet ache and
no matter how much my shoulders hurt and
no matter how much I want to strangle
the snoring old man across the [ __ ]
bunks from me, I'm going to miss all of
it. Trying to keep that in mind and
pushing forward. So, [music] but I can't
wait to get back to the mountains.
Honestly, I don't think I'll have
another chance to make one of these
before then. So, I'll see [music] you in
the Alps.
>> [music]
[music]
[music]
[music]
>> Heat. Heat. [music]
[music]
Heat. Heat.
[music]
[music]
>> [music]
[music]
>> Okay, it's October 18th. I have [music]
13 days left in my trip. If I look
miserable, it's because I kind of am.
Yeah, I I I kind of had a a little bit
of a [ __ ] up with like scheduling my
stays and everything because the trains
yesterday to go to Shaman from where I
am just like didn't exist basically. So,
which means that I've been in Schwitz,
Switzerland for three full days. And
this place is the most unremarkable
place I have been in this entire trip.
I'm not even kidding. This might be
worse than Budapest. on top of
everything from like there being nothing
in this town, the weather being [ __ ]
there's no one in this hostel. There's
nobody. The cherry on top of all that is
it's expensive as [ __ ] for no reason.
[sighs]
Yeah, the past 3 days have sucked. I'm
not going to lie. I uh I actually called
it quits on a hike today, which I've
never done. This is the first day the
sun has been out the entire time I' I've
been in Schwitz. And I went to go do the
only hike that I even came to this town
for. And yeah, there's [ __ ] a ton of
people on that hike. Extremely popular
hike. I mean, it was Yeah, it wasn't
there. I was not able to get any shots
with just me in it. I didn't even try to
be honest cuz it was that bad.
Definitely thinking about home a lot,
especially in the hard moments. But um I
don't know. [snorts]
The next day, I was more than happy to
finally leave that place and head for
Shyaman, France.
I was a little concerned because the
weather wasn't looking too good, but
this was another instance where it ended
up being some of the best days of the
trip, solely because of the people that
I met there.
The weather sucked for basically the
entire time I was in Shamani except for
legitimately a single hour of the second
day where the weather cleared up just
for us to go up to take a look at Mlancc
at around 12,000 ft.
[music]
[music]
>> [music]
>> This cameras
[music]
[music]
[music]
and And I'm really glad that it ended on
that note cuz this was my last
destination in the mountains. After
this, I would be heading for Geneva and
then Paris and then London and then that
was that. After what felt like a pretty
long trip from Paris to London, I
arrived at my nice hotel to give myself
a bit of a break from these hostiles.
And um yeah, my trip had come to an end.
Oh my god, dude. This is honestly like
such a weird This is such a strange
feeling. I'm like I never thought I
would see home in this light where I
miss it so much. Even though like just
over 2 months ago I was like losing my
mind and I hated it there. But I just I
can't believe I haven't been home in
basically 58 59 days at this point.
Yeah, this is this has been this has
been a crazy like 2 months. I've never
ever done anything even remotely like
this in my entire life.
One of the things I discovered about
myself is that I'm just kind of like
chill. I don't know. I always like I I
always saw myself as someone who kind of
[ __ ] up under pressure. But every
single instance in this entire trip
where it was like, "Oh [ __ ] I [music]
missed this train." Or, "Oh [ __ ] this
got cancelled."
The anxiety was there for like a couple
seconds and it was gone.
I was just completely confident in my
ability that like no matter what
happens, I'll figure it out. There was
never a moment where I I [music] started
to like panic. And I mean that's a
lesson that I've learned or lesson that
I've kind of already been aware of. And
so I guess a lesson that like people
sometimes don't really realize. It's
like there's no reason to freak out in
any case scenario.
>> [music]
>> You absolutely 100% underestimate your
ability to just figure it out.
>> I'm going up there.
>> I didn't think that I would be 22, I
think, when I would do something like
this crazy. Um, I thought that I would
need more real life experience before
doing it. I I wouldn't say I have all
that much life experience here at 22
years old. And I went out to Europe with
zero of plan and I figured it the [ __ ]
out and it everything was fine.
It sounds cliche cuz I'm sure you've
heard a million Tik Toks saying the same
dumb [ __ ] but everyone says it cuz it's
true. You are so much more capable than
you think you are. Trust me. Trust me,
you can.
[music]
I just I never I never thought I [music]
couldn't have a YouTube channel, a
successful YouTube channel. I never
thought I couldn't travel to Europe by
myself. [music]
>> It was just a matter of whether I
actually pursued it or not.
[music]
